questions.question: Highlight a detail from Excerpt 1 that shows an obstacle or challenge Hatshepsut faced as a woman in power.

responses.content: Hatshepsut, royal daughter of Pharaoh Tuthmosis and his Great Wife Ahmose, grew up in an Egypt that was peaceful, prosperous, and respected throughout the known world.

Therefore, the response is incorrect.

responses.content: At first, little Tuthmosis III was considered the pharaoh, with Hatshepsut just his second-in-command. But a small child could not be an effective ruler
responses.bucket_rationale: The student's most recent highlight is: 'At first, little Tuthmosis III was considered the pharaoh, with Hatshepsut just his second-in-command. But a small child could not be an effective ruler'. This does not directly show Hatshepsut taking action to empower herself. It only sets up the situation, not her actions. questions.question: Highlight a detail from Excerpt 2 that shows Elena taking action to empower herself.

responses.content: There is no reliable record of exactly when or how it happened, but at some point
responses.bucket_rationale: The student's highlight, "There is no reliable record of exactly when or how it happened, but at some point," does not answer the question. The question asks for a detail that shows an obstacle or challenge Hatshepsut faced as a woman in power. The highlighted text is about the uncertainty of when Hatshepsut became pharaoh, not about the challenges she faced as a woman. It does not meet the requirements of the question.

responses.content: There was no word in the language of ancient Egypt for a female ruler; a queen was simply the wife of a king. Hatshepsut had no choice: she had to call herself pharaoh, or king—a male title. She was concerned with preserving and continuing traditional order as much as possible, so to the people of Egypt she made herself look like a man in her role as pharaoh. In ceremonies, she wore a man’s short kilt instead of a woman’s long dress, much as she had as a child. Around her neck she wore a king’s broad collar. She even fastened a false golden beard to her chin. When she wrote about herself as pharaoh, sometimes she referred to herself as he, other times as she. This would be very confusing for historians
responses.bucket_rationale: The student's highlight is from the correct section and includes the key detail about Hatshepsut facing the challenge of there being no word for a female ruler, and how she had to take on a male title and appearance. The highlight is a bit long, but it is focused on the specific challenge and does not exceed a reasonable length for a fourth-grade response. The content directly answers the question about an obstacle Hatshepsut faced as a woman in power.

questions.question: Highlight a detail from Excerpt 2 that shows an obstacle or challenge Elena faces in her pursuit of learning English.

responses.content:
Spanish isn’t good enough.

               I remember how I’d smile

               listening to my little ones,

               understanding every word they’d say,

(5)       their jokes, their songs, their plots.

               Vamos a pedirle dulces a mamá. Vamos.

               But that was in Mexico.

               Now my children go to American high schools.

               They speak English. At night they sit around

(10)      the kitchen table, laugh with one another.

               I stand at the stove and feel dumb, alone.

               I bought a book to learn English.

               My husband frowned, drank more beer.

               My oldest said, “Mamá, he doesn’t want you

(15)      to be smarter than he is.” I’m forty,

               embarrassed at mispronouncing words,

               embarrassed at the laughter of my children,

                the grocer, the mailman. Sometimes I take

                my English book and lock myself in the bathroom,

(20)      say the thick words softly,

               for if I stop trying, I will be deaf

               when my children need my help.

The student's response exceeds the character limit and does not meet the requirements of the question.
